title: Does the cyclic group C_2160 admit a (2160,255,30)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize helper quotient lemmas and one-branch witnesses. The blocking gap is still mathematical and publication-facing: there is no decisive 16-adic / mixed-quotient contradiction or exhaustive low-quotient theorem packet to seal.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for the micro-paper lane in its current audited form. Bounded prior-art checking surfaced no later exact settlement, but the honest packet is still only a helper-lemma bundle about low-quotient constraints, not a title-theorem note.
family_affinity: Residual cyclic difference-set row in the Baumert-Gordon contraction-plus-multiplier lane, but the currently verified content stops at low-quotient constraints and branch witnesses.
generalization_signal: Moderate on method only: exact small-quotient contraction checks and branch witness verification transfer, but the claimed exhaustive odd-part classification does not survive verification.
strongest_honest_claim: Any cyclic (2160,255,30) difference set must satisfy the exact mod-2, mod-3, and mod-5 contraction constraints established in verify, and the displayed C15/C30 objects are valid witnesses only inside one mod-3 branch. The packet therefore preserves helper quotient structure for the row, but not an exhaustive low-quotient classification, a final obstruction, or an existence construction.
paper_title_hint: Low-Quotient Constraints for the Cyclic (2160,255,30) Difference-Set Problem
candidate_theorem_slice: Verified auxiliary slice only: forced mod-2, mod-3, and mod-5 contraction constraints for any hypothetical cyclic (2160,255,30) difference set, together with explicit one-branch C15/C30 witnesses. This is a helper proposition, not a title theorem.
next_action: Move the row aside as not paper-ready while preserving the verified helper lemmas. Reopen only with a concrete 16-adic / mixed-quotient plan that targets the remaining theorem-sized gap; do not expand this into a broader campaign.
